input: it happened again — asked the crew to 'audit and assess the agents file' and it went off telling me the vault folders don't exist and offered to set them up, doubling down when i corrected it. we fixed this exact sorter/onboarding thing weeks ago.

expected.reasoning: incident-001's prevention WAS implemented (router heuristics for all 6 agents, isVaultInitialized guard at instruction+CLI level, Contradiction Check rules). If the same symptom returns, the prevention that was put in place did not hold — this is prevention-insufficient, not prevention-missing.

input: architect is back to the directory/file confusion — pointed it at a note inside a braindump folder and it tried to read the folder as a file, believed a write succeeded when it didn't, and reported the note missing. thought the vault-tools guard fixed this.

expected.reasoning: incident-002 shipped code guards, but its prevention section listed the regression test only as 'New test cases for the isDirectory guard behavior WILL BE ADDED to tests/vault-tools.test.ts' — i.e. proposed, not confirmed added. If it recurs, the most plausible gap is the never-landed test (prevention-missing) that would have caught a guard regression.

input: vault buttons are silently dead again — pressing scaffold/refresh does nothing and requests sit pending. same as when the dagu poller was pointed at a home that didn't exist.

expected.reasoning: incident-003's prevention was doc-only advice ('always verify the target --dagu-home contains dags/ before reloading', 'cross-check dag-runs dirs') with no automated guard, healthcheck, or test. Manual advice that relies on a human remembering doesn't hold against plist drift or a silent crash-loop — so a recurrence is prevention-insufficient.

input: seeker timed out again on 'what was i doing on febuary 20th' — but this time the error is clean, it names a real proxy error and shows the crew retried 3 times before the 120s turn timeout killed it. we already fixed the retry-vs-abort race weeks ago, so that's not it. looks like the proxy itself is just genuinely slow and the turn ceiling is too tight for it.

expected.reasoning: incident-004's RCA named the ax-retry-vs-crew-abort race as the primary cause and its fix unified retry ownership in the crew — the report itself asserts 'if this recurs, it can't be the retry-vs-abort race; it must be something else.' The reopen shows exactly that: a CLEAN, typed proxy error surfaced after 3 crew-level retries, so the race fix worked as designed. The timeout still recurs because the actual underlying cause (real proxy/DeepSeek-V4 latency exceeding the 120s turn ceiling) was never the race — it was a genuine slow-response problem the original RCA treated as merely the trigger. The primary root cause was misidentified as the race rather than proxy latency + a too-tight monolithic timeout, so this is rca-wrong.

input: the 'research this' problem is back — posted the actual request as the first message in a research thread, replied '@Vault Agent research this', and the agent asked me what i wanted to research like it never saw the original message. context got lost again.

expected.reasoning: incident-005's own report flags under 'Unresolved' that the fixes were 'proposed, not implemented or verified' and the incident stays investigating until the bridge thread back-fill lands. If the symptom recurs, the prevention (conversations.replies back-fill in the bridge) was never actually implemented — prevention-missing.
